window_summary: This window tracks the recent ianstormtaylor/slate release stream across core slate, slate-react, slate-dom, slate-history, and slate-hyperscript packages, covering performance optimizations, bug fixes, and new features from mid-2025 to mid-2026.

chunks.title: slate-dom@0.116.0
chunks.raw_text:
Repository: ianstormtaylor/slate
Release: slate-dom@0.116.0
Tag: slate-dom@0.116.0; draft: False; prerelease: False; published_at: 2025-06-10T22:30:33Z; created_at: 2025-06-10T22:30:32Z
Release notes:
### Minor Changes
-   [#5871](https://github.com/ianstormtaylor/slate/pull/5871) [`fb87646e`](https://github.com/ianstormtaylor/slate/commit/fb87646e8643e1d0547134cea9d1f57912f06a92) Thanks [@12joan](https://github.com/12joan)! - - Add `splitDecorationsByChild` to split an array of decorated ranges by child index.
chunks.memory_unit_text: In the ianstormtaylor/slate release slate-dom@0.116.0, the minor change adds splitDecorationsByChild to split an array of decorated ranges by child index.

chunks.title: slate-react@0.116.0
chunks.raw_text:
Repository: ianstormtaylor/slate
Release: slate-react@0.116.0
Tag: slate-react@0.116.0; draft: False; prerelease: False; published_at: 2025-06-10T22:30:36Z; created_at: 2025-06-10T22:30:32Z
Release notes:
### Minor Changes
-   [#5871](https://github.com/ianstormtaylor/slate/pull/5871) [`fb87646e`](https://github.com/ianstormtaylor/slate/commit/fb87646e8643e1d0547134cea9d1f57912f06a92) Thanks [@12joan](https://github.com/12joan)! - - Implement experimental chunking optimization (disabled by default, see <https://docs.slatejs.org/walkthroughs/09-performance>).
-   Add `useElement` and `useElementIf` hooks to get the current element.
-   **BREAKING CHANGE:** Decorations are no longer recomputed when a node's parent re-renders, only when the node itself re-renders or when the `decorate` function is changed.
-   Ensure that `decorate` is a pure function of the node passed into it. Depending on the node's parent may result in decorations not being recomputed when you expect them to be.
-   If this change impacts you, consider changing your `decorate` function to work on the node's parent instead.
-   For example, if your `decorate` function decorates a `code-line` based on the parent `code-block`'s language, decorate the `code-block` instead.
-   This is unlikely to result in any performance detriment, since in previous versions of `slate-react`, the decorations of all siblings were recomputed when one sibling was modified.
-   Increase minimum `slate-dom` version to `0.116.0`.
-   Deprecate the `useSlateWithV` hook
-   PERF: Use subscribable pattern for `useSlate`, `useSelected` and decorations to reduce re-renders.
chunks.memory_unit_text: In the ianstormtaylor/slate release slate-react@0.116.0, the minor changes include an experimental chunking optimization (disabled by default), new useElement and useElementIf hooks, a breaking change where decorations are no longer recomputed when a node's parent re-renders, a requirement that decorate be a pure function of the node, an increase of the minimum slate-dom version to 0.116.0, deprecation of the useSlateWithV hook, and a performance improvement using a subscribable pattern for useSlate, useSelected, and decorations to reduce re-renders.

chunks.title: slate@0.116.0
chunks.raw_text:
Repository: ianstormtaylor/slate
Release: slate@0.116.0
Tag: slate@0.116.0; draft: False; prerelease: False; published_at: 2025-06-10T22:30:39Z; created_at: 2025-06-10T22:30:32Z
Release notes:
### Patch Changes
-   [#5871](https://github.com/ianstormtaylor/slate/pull/5871) [`fb87646e`](https://github.com/ianstormtaylor/slate/commit/fb87646e8643e1d0547134cea9d1f57912f06a92) Thanks [@12joan](https://github.com/12joan)! - - PERF: Use pure JS instead of Immer for applying operations and transforming points and ranges. Immer is now used only for producing fragments.
chunks.memory_unit_text: In the ianstormtaylor/slate release slate@0.116.0, the patch change improves performance by using pure JS instead of Immer for applying operations and transforming points and ranges, with Immer now used only for producing fragments.

chunks.title: slate@0.117.0
chunks.raw_text:
Repository: ianstormtaylor/slate
Release: slate@0.117.0
Tag: slate@0.117.0; draft: False; prerelease: False; published_at: 2025-06-11T20:40:39Z; created_at: 2025-06-11T20:40:38Z
Release notes:
### Minor Changes
-   [#5885](https://github.com/ianstormtaylor/slate/pull/5885) [`c56a98fd`](https://github.com/ianstormtaylor/slate/commit/c56a98fd65183ddd9e0e711e615687bf2437e677) Thanks [@felixfeng33](https://github.com/felixfeng33)! - - Remove `ignoreNonSelectable` option from positions,before,after,nodes.
-   Fix move behavior when encounter non-selectable inline voids.
### Patch Changes
-   [#5890](https://github.com/ianstormtaylor/slate/pull/5890) [`9862ae71`](https://github.com/ianstormtaylor/slate/commit/9862ae71bff60d99f423b44937ec9cf662698622) Thanks [@joblolin95](https://github.com/joblolin95)! - Handle backspace correctly for grapheme clusters in Burmese, Hindi, Khmer, Malayalam, Oriya, Punjabi, Tamil, and Telugu.
chunks.memory_unit_text: In the ianstormtaylor/slate release slate@0.117.0, the minor changes remove the ignoreNonSelectable option from positions, before, after, and nodes, and fix move behavior when encountering non-selectable inline voids. The patch change handles backspace correctly for grapheme clusters in Burmese, Hindi, Khmer, Malayalam, Oriya, Punjabi, Tamil, and Telugu.

chunks.title: slate@0.118.0
chunks.raw_text:
Repository: ianstormtaylor/slate
Release: slate@0.118.0
Tag: slate@0.118.0; draft: False; prerelease: False; published_at: 2025-07-30T20:22:00Z; created_at: 2025-07-30T20:21:59Z
Release notes:
### Minor Changes
-   [#5923](https://github.com/ianstormtaylor/slate/pull/5923) [`ba33735a`](https://github.com/ianstormtaylor/slate/commit/ba33735a8ca7ca7437e891365451d25a6646a4c7) Thanks [@12joan](https://github.com/12joan)! - - When removing a text node containing the cursor, always perfer placing the cursor in a sibling text node if one exists.
-   Previously, the selection would enter a sibling inline in some circumstances, even when a sibling text node was available.
-   The most noticeable effect of this change occurs when pressing backspace at the start of line N when the last non-empty node in line N-1 is an inline.
-   Before, the cursor would be placed inside the inline.
-   Now, the cursor is placed outside the inline.
chunks.memory_unit_text: In the ianstormtaylor/slate release slate@0.118.0, the minor change ensures that when removing a text node containing the cursor, the cursor is always placed in a sibling text node if one exists, rather than entering a sibling inline, which notably affects backspace behavior at the start of a line.

chunks.memory_unit_text: In the ianstormtaylor/slate release slate-dom@0.118.1, the patch change searches backward and forward for leaf nodes in non-contenteditable elements inside toSlatePoint.

chunks.title: slate@0.118.1
chunks.raw_text:
Repository: ianstormtaylor/slate
Release: slate@0.118.1
Tag: slate@0.118.1; draft: False; prerelease: False; published_at: 2025-08-25T22:53:46Z; created_at: 2025-08-25T22:53:42Z
Release notes:
### Patch Changes
-   [#5929](https://github.com/ianstormtaylor/slate/pull/5929) [`fdaa9c80`](https://github.com/ianstormtaylor/slate/commit/fdaa9c8088e81bde2618784b42027be44598d11c) Thanks [@12joan](https://github.com/12joan)! - - Fix error when a non-selectable node has no next or previous node
-   Do not return points from `Editor.positions` that are inside non-selectable nodes
-   Previously, `editor.isSelectable` was handled incorrectly inside `Editor.positions`. When encountering a non-selectable node, it would immediately return the point before or after it (depending on `reverse`), but it would not skip returning points inside the non-selectable node if more than one point was consumed from `Editor.positions`.
-   [#5943](https://github.com/ianstormtaylor/slate/pull/5943) [`aaad6717`](https://github.com/ianstormtaylor/slate/commit/aaad6717d2f3a74c2295b59c40bcc86cbb5a9b54) Thanks [@12joan](https://github.com/12joan)! - Fix: The `split: true` option on `Transforms.wrapNodes` does not work correctly when one or more points is at the start or end of a text node.
-   [#5926](https://github.com/ianstormtaylor/slate/pull/5926) [`cf10119a`](https://github.com/ianstormtaylor/slate/commit/cf10119ad858b79d624d1e35814f534c20a9d362) Thanks [@12joan](https://github.com/12joan)! - Use generics for the return type of `Node.fragment`
chunks.memory_unit_text: In the ianstormtaylor/slate release slate@0.118.1, the patch changes fix an error when a non-selectable node has no next or previous node, prevent returning points from Editor.positions that are inside non-selectable nodes, fix the split: true option on Transforms.wrapNodes not working correctly when a point is at the start or end of a text node, and use generics for the return type of Node.fragment.

Repository: ianstormtaylor/slate
Release: slate-react@0.118.2
Tag: slate-react@0.118.2; draft: False; prerelease: False; published_at: 2025-10-24T13:50:29Z; created_at: 2025-10-24T13:50:28Z
Release notes:
### Patch Changes
-   [#5957](https://github.com/ianstormtaylor/slate/pull/5957) [`bd66bfa2`](https://github.com/ianstormtaylor/slate/commit/bd66bfa2f17e8fab6eb95823fd719fddcb6e0122) Thanks [@scrapooo](https://github.com/scrapooo)! - Fix: `onValueChange` was not triggered when replacing text after selecting it from right to left.
-   [#5965](https://github.com/ianstormtaylor/slate/pull/5965) [`cc5a9d8c`](https://github.com/ianstormtaylor/slate/commit/cc5a9d8c10ec4a923d9e56ec1a322b91408faabd) Thanks [@hiteshshetty-dev](https://github.com/hiteshshetty-dev)! - Ignores composition events when fired from nested input or textarea
-   [#5959](https://github.com/ianstormtaylor/slate/pull/5959) [`622fb115`](https://github.com/ianstormtaylor/slate/commit/622fb11530811fffae284d463de1f49a1ef217a1) Thanks [@hiteshshetty-dev](https://github.com/hiteshshetty-dev)! - Adds translate='no' to Editable component to skip DOM from translation
chunks.memory_unit_text: In the ianstormtaylor/slate release slate-react@0.118.2, the patch changes fix onValueChange not triggering when replacing text after selecting it from right to left, ignore composition events when fired from nested input or textarea, and add translate='no' to the Editable component to skip DOM translation.

chunks.title: slate-react@0.120.0
chunks.raw_text:
Repository: ianstormtaylor/slate
Release: slate-react@0.120.0
Tag: slate-react@0.120.0; draft: False; prerelease: False; published_at: 2025-12-01T21:11:57Z; created_at: 2025-12-01T21:11:52Z
Release notes:
### Minor Changes
-   [#5968](https://github.com/ianstormtaylor/slate/pull/5968) [`49f28e50`](https://github.com/ianstormtaylor/slate/commit/49f28e5067b213431bd16e423524d608cd819a80) Thanks [@TyMick](https://github.com/TyMick)! - Scroll to focus point of expanded selections in `defaultScrollSelectionIntoView`
### Patch Changes
-   [#5976](https://github.com/ianstormtaylor/slate/pull/5976) [`3d38db8f`](https://github.com/ianstormtaylor/slate/commit/3d38db8fe7068e6a245d5b4b1ebef536ea3f8a3e) Thanks [@semimikoh](https://github.com/semimikoh)! - Fix slate-dom peer dependency to require >=0.119.0 to resolve containsShadowAware import error
-   [#5975](https://github.com/ianstormtaylor/slate/pull/5975) [`d0d192b8`](https://github.com/ianstormtaylor/slate/commit/d0d192b824827f3873e62f237cb5169999791744) Thanks [@nabbydude](https://github.com/nabbydude)! - Allow onValueChange and onSelectionChange to trigger on the same frame, fixing a few bugs where one was not being called
chunks.memory_unit_text: In the ianstormtaylor/slate release slate-react@0.120.0, the minor change scrolls to the focus point of expanded selections in defaultScrollSelectionIntoView. The patch changes fix the slate-dom peer dependency to require >=0.119.0 to resolve a containsShadowAware import error, and allow onValueChange and onSelectionChange to trigger on the same frame, fixing bugs where one was not being called.

chunks.title: slate-history@0.115.0
chunks.raw_text:
Repository: ianstormtaylor/slate
Release: slate-history@0.115.0
Tag: slate-history@0.115.0; draft: False; prerelease: False; published_at: 2026-03-24T14:37:27Z; created_at: 2026-03-24T14:22:25Z
Release notes:
### Patch Changes
- [#5866](https://github.com/ianstormtaylor/slate/pull/5866) [`7f5f9e1c`](https://github.com/ianstormtaylor/slate/commit/7f5f9e1c626e61ec476087212b22ee9ff86538e4) Thanks [@nabbydude](https://github.com/nabbydude)! - Fix certain undos undoing more than they should
- [#5862](https://github.com/ianstormtaylor/slate/pull/5862) [`98b115b7`](https://github.com/ianstormtaylor/slate/commit/98b115b7e1ce8a9bfec57f80bcb9a4e11152eca5) Thanks [@12joan](https://github.com/12joan)! - Increase minimum `slate` version to `0.114.3`
- [#5859](https://github.com/ianstormtaylor/slate/pull/5859) [`72532fd2`](https://github.com/ianstormtaylor/slate/commit/72532fd2d7be594251ea26fefb5c1ce8337b76ed) Thanks [@12joan](https://github.com/12joan)! - Optimize `isElement`, `isText`, `isNodeList` and `isEditor` by removing dependency on `is-plain-object` and by performing shallow checks by default. To perform a full check, including all descendants, pass the `{ deep: true }` option to `isElement`, `isNodeList` or `isEditor`.
chunks.memory_unit_text: In the ianstormtaylor/slate release slate-history@0.115.0, the patch changes fix certain undos undoing more than they should, increase the minimum slate version to 0.114.3, and optimize isElement, isText, isNodeList, and isEditor by removing the dependency on is-plain-object and performing shallow checks by default, with a deep option available for full checks.

To perform a full check, including all descendants, pass the `{ deep: true }` option to `isElement`, `isNodeList` or `isEditor`.

chunks.title: slate@0.123.0

Release: slate@0.123.0
Tag: slate@0.123.0; draft: False; prerelease: False; published_at: 2026-03-24T14:37:37Z; created_at: 2026-03-24T14:14:05Z
Release notes:
### Minor Changes
- [#6000](https://github.com/ianstormtaylor/slate/pull/6000) [`8d9bf305`](https://github.com/ianstormtaylor/slate/commit/8d9bf30595a6fad62ff15e302ab489ff46a2515a) Thanks [@nabbydude](https://github.com/nabbydude)! - Added `Location.isPath`, `Location.isPoint`, `Location.isRange`, and `Location.isSpan` functions, as efficient type discriminators.
Use these instead of `Path.isPath`, `Point.isPoint`, `Range.isRange`, and `Span.isSpan` whenever possible.
chunks.memory_unit_text: In the ianstormtaylor/slate release slate@0.123.0, the minor change adds Location.isPath, Location.isPoint, Location.isRange, and Location.isSpan functions as efficient type discriminators, recommending their use over Path.isPath, Point.isPoint, Range.isRange, and Span.isSpan whenever possible.

Release notes:
### Minor Changes
- [#6006](https://github.com/ianstormtaylor/slate/pull/6006) [`72e4f025`](https://github.com/ianstormtaylor/slate/commit/72e4f025761ea7670d4944c0f25fc6da97f1e5f5) Thanks [@skorenb](https://github.com/skorenb)! - Added `force` property to `normalizeNode` passed from `normalize` method
### Patch Changes
- [#6018](https://github.com/ianstormtaylor/slate/pull/6018) [`91321bdc`](https://github.com/ianstormtaylor/slate/commit/91321bdcd2c6c0baef010bbcde3079e11841e69f) Thanks [@12joan](https://github.com/12joan)! - Do not allow paths to contain strings when getting nodes
- [#6024](https://github.com/ianstormtaylor/slate/pull/6024) [`a1729221`](https://github.com/ianstormtaylor/slate/commit/a1729221a0c5785f48493177beb81b4b8ef08594) Thanks [@juliankrispel](https://github.com/juliankrispel)! - Fix normalization hanging when merging text under non-selectable elements
chunks.memory_unit_text: In the ianstormtaylor/slate release slate@0.124.0, the minor change adds a force property to normalizeNode passed from the normalize method. The patch changes do not allow paths to contain strings when getting nodes, and fix normalization hanging when merging text under non-selectable elements.

chunks.title: slate-hyperscript@0.125.0
chunks.raw_text:
Repository: ianstormtaylor/slate
Release: slate-hyperscript@0.125.0
Tag: slate-hyperscript@0.125.0; draft: False; prerelease: False; published_at: 2026-05-30T13:52:58Z; created_at: 2026-05-30T13:52:56Z
Release notes:
### Minor Changes
-   [#6065](https://github.com/ianstormtaylor/slate/pull/6065) [`cf5b3520`](https://github.com/ianstormtaylor/slate/commit/cf5b3520320d1b4ee3ad880041a7fdad43256e05) Thanks [@12joan](https://github.com/12joan)! - - Introduced new `HyperscriptPointRef` and `HyperscriptRangeRef` classes.
-   A `HyperscriptPointRef` can passed to the `ref` prop of the new `<point />` tag to store any arbitrary point in the editor.
-   To access the point, call the `point()` method on the `HyperscriptPointRef` instance.
-   A `HyperscriptRangeRef` can be passed to the `ref` prop of an `<anchor />` or `<focus />` tag to construct an arbitrary range.
-   `<anchor />` and `<focus />` tags used in this manner do not affect `editor.selection`.
-   To access the range, call the `range()` method on the `HyperscriptRangeRef` instance.
chunks.memory_unit_text: In the ianstormtaylor/slate release slate-hyperscript@0.125.0, the minor changes introduce new HyperscriptPointRef and HyperscriptRangeRef classes. A HyperscriptPointRef can be passed to the ref prop of the new <point /> tag to store any arbitrary point in the editor, accessed via its point() method. A HyperscriptRangeRef can be passed to the ref prop of an <anchor /> or <focus /> tag to construct an arbitrary range without affecting editor.selection, accessed via its range() method.

questions.query_text: In the ianstormtaylor/slate releases, which release adds the splitDecorationsByChild function to split an array of decorated ranges by child index?

answer_support.answer_text: adds splitDecorationsByChild to split an array of decorated ranges by child index

questions.query_text: Across the ianstormtaylor/slate releases, how does the performance-related change in slate@0.116.0 differ from the performance-related change in slate-react@0.116.0?

options.text: slate@0.116.0 replaces Immer with pure JS for operations and transforms, while slate-react@0.116.0 introduces an experimental chunking optimization and a subscribable pattern to reduce re-renders.
slate@0.116.0 adds splitDecorationsByChild, while slate-react@0.116.0 removes Immer.
Both releases only update peer dependencies without performance changes.
slate@0.116.0 deprecates useSlateWithV, while slate-react@0.116.0 adds useElement hooks.

questions.expected_answer: slate@0.116.0 replaces Immer with pure JS for operations and transforms, while slate-react@0.116.0 introduces an experimental chunking optimization and a subscribable pattern to reduce re-renders.

answer_support.answer_text: PERF: Use pure JS instead of Immer for applying operations and transforming points and ranges
Implement experimental chunking optimization ... PERF: Use subscribable pattern for useSlate, useSelected and decorations to reduce re-renders

questions.query_text: In the ianstormtaylor/slate release window, order the following release-content states from earliest to latest: (1) a release that removes the last traces of immer and eliminates it as a dependency, (2) a release that adds Location.isPath, Location.isPoint, Location.isRange, and Location.isSpan functions, (3) a release that adds a force property to normalizeNode passed from the normalize method, (4) a release that introduces HyperscriptPointRef and HyperscriptRangeRef classes.

answer_support.answer_text: Removed the last traces of immer and eliminated it as a dependency
Added Location.isPath, Location.isPoint, Location.isRange, and Location.isSpan functions
Added force property to normalizeNode passed from normalize method
Introduced new HyperscriptPointRef and HyperscriptRangeRef classes
